Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

this looks like it is failing consistently in sanity test_101d, which is a readahead test.  It appears that this is totally disabling readahead, because all of the "readahead" cases are now slower than "no-readahead"?

Patch Set 3:

Andreas, I post this patch as discussion start with Patrik.
I understand - problem is more serrious than this patch.
I had post some details in the ticket.

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1
> 
> this looks like it is failing consistently in sanity test_101d, which is a readahead test.  It appears that this is totally disabling readahead, because all of the "readahead" cases are now slower than "no-readahead"?

Andreas, as I see - 4M is default stripe size now? it's right? but It mean many many sanity.sh tests don't fixed to read a different stripe.

Patch Set 3:

> Andreas, as I see - 4M is default stripe size now? it's right? but
 > It mean many many sanity.sh tests don't fixed to read a different
 > stripe.

It's definitely possible that there are tests which are not failing, but are not working as expected. If you find such tests then it would be good to fix them. 

We've been fixing some tests to allow the default file layout to change during testing, so that eventually it is possible to set a default PFL layout on the filesystem at mkfs time, since that would make life easier for users. Unfortunately, there are still a lot of tests that will fail with anything other than a plain 1-stripe layout, so more work is needed.

Abandoned

replaced with new patch

Patch Set 3:

https://review.whamcloud.com/53635

commit.subject: LU-16362 lov: dont't allow RA outside of active stripe
commit.message:
LU-16362 lov: dont't allow RA outside of active stripe

don't allow a readahead to be against of inactive stripe,
it counfuded an invariants checking and caused
page uncovered lock panic.

HPe-bug-id: LUS-11367
Signed-off-by: Alexey Lyashkov <alexey.lyashkov@hpe.com>
Change-Id: I5ef9b2f29a539e84752eeae81c64b51ffb706754
